hypothesis: If Fe K-edge XAS spectra of iron-containing proteins are accurately digitized from the literature and paired with curated local structural graphs, then the resulting standardized dataset will both reproduce ground-truth spectra with low error and high inter-expert agreement and enable machine-learning models to predict spectra from structure and coordination number/nearest-neighbor distance from spectra significantly better than random baselines, because XANES/EXAFS features encode local bonding geometry and the curation pipeline preserves this information.
